rmn_001.title: Supply Run
rmn_001.description: The Remnant needs organics. Bad harvest season across three sectors. We're not asking for charity — we're asking for a trader who won't report us to Helix.
dialogue_intro: You found us. That's either resourcefulness or desperation.
Either works. We need 50 tons of organics, sector 321, no questions asked.
Helix has a price on anything heading our way. Your problem, not mine.
We pay fair. 120 per ton, plus 2,000 hazard bonus if you make it clean.
dialogue_complete: On time. Undeclared. I'm impressed.
The colonists on Remnant's Hope will eat this winter. That matters.
You've got more runs if you want them. We can use someone with your nerve.

rmn_002.title: Clear the Lane
rmn_002.description: Apex has a patrol sitting on the warp link between sectors 321 and 350. Two enforcer-class ships. Our supply convoys can't move while they're there.
dialogue_intro: We have a problem with three hundred tons of food and nowhere to put it.
Apex has two Enforcers parked at the 321-350 junction. Toll collectors with guns.
I need them gone. Not negotiated with. Gone.
Twenty thousand credits and my personal guarantee: you'll have friends in every Remnant sector after this.
dialogue_complete: Clean work. The convoy got through this morning.
Apex will replace those patrols eventually. But we'll be watching.
You're not just a trader anymore, Korr. Whether you like it or not.

drift_001.title: Blind Delivery
drift_001.description: You don't ask what's in it. I don't tell you. You pick it up, you drop it off, you walk away richer. Simple business.
dialogue_intro: Word travels. You're the pilot who told Apex to go to hell.
I respect that. Professionally speaking.
I've got a cargo that needs to move from 241 to 290. Twenty units. Light.
Twelve thousand flat. Don't open it. Don't scan it. Don't be late.
dialogue_complete: Right package, right sector, right time. You're better at this than most.
The Cartel has work if you want it. Good work. Paid work.
And Korr — what's in those packages? Medical supplies. Seriously. Helix has a monopoly, we don't.

iron_001.title: Salvage Recovery
iron_001.description: One of our survey ships went dark near the Fracture Zone boundary. We need the salvage data it was carrying. The ship is in sector 399.
dialogue_intro: I'll be direct with you. We lost a ship three weeks ago.
Survey vessel. Entered sector 399 to chart the Fracture boundary. Never came back.
We've recovered the signal — the ship is there, drifting. Hull intact.
We need the survey data. Not the ship. Just what's in the data core.
Fifteen thousand credits. Don't linger at 399 longer than necessary.
dialogue_complete: The data is... extensive. More than we expected.
I won't tell you what it says. Ironveil policy. But I'll tell you this: your hazard bonus is doubled.
And stay away from sector 399 for a while. Consider that professional advice.

ghost_001.title: The Signal
ghost_001.description: A repeating transmission from inside the Fracture Zone. The coordinates it provides lead to sector 420. No one who has followed it before has reported back.
dialogue_intro: [SIGNAL-VII // FRACTURE ZONE // AUTHENTICATED]
WE REMEMBER THE PILOT WHO WOULD NOT FIRE.
WE HAVE WAITED FOR ONE WHO COULD CHOOSE.
COORDINATES: SECTOR 420 // APPROACH ALONE // WE WILL NOT FIRE.
[END TRANSMISSION]
dialogue_complete: [SIGNAL-VII // VERIFICATION COMPLETE]
YOU CAME. THIS IS NOTED.
THE FRACTURE WAS NOT AN ACCIDENT.
WHEN YOU ARE READY TO UNDERSTAND, RETURN.
[END TRANSMISSION]

helix_001.title: Pharmaceutical Run
helix_001.description: Off the record: a colony in sector 85 has an outbreak. The official Helix response will take three weeks. If you move medicine there in the next four days, I'll make sure you're compensated.
dialogue_intro: This isn't an official Helix request. This is me, personally.
There's a colony outbreak in sector 85. Respiratory. Sixty affected, twelve critical.
The official channels are... slow. Politics. You know how it is.
I need forty units of medicine there before the situation becomes irreversible.
I'll pay from my own discretionary fund. Eight thousand credits. Nobody hears about this.
dialogue_complete: They'll survive. All of them, I think.
You should know — Helix knew about the outbreak before I did.
The delay was intentional. Market pressure on that colony's mining contract.
I'm telling you this because you should know who you're dealing with when you trade with us.

apex_001.title: Old Debts
apex_001.description: Voss has found you. She has a proposition — not a request. Eliminate a Drift Cartel operation that's been hitting Apex supply lines. Or she files the full report on Sector 7.
dialogue_intro: Don't reach for anything. I'm alone.
I know where you've been, Korr. Every sector. Every port.
The Sector 7 incident report is sitting on my desk. Incomplete.
There's a Cartel operation in sector 260 hitting our supply lines. Eight runs in six weeks.
Shut it down. Three ships. Then we're square and that report stays incomplete.
This isn't a negotiation.
dialogue_complete: Efficient. Almost nostalgic.
The report stays filed. Incomplete.
Don't mistake this for warmth, Korr. I'm investing in a useful asset.
When I need you again, you'll hear from me.
